Master’s Programme in Translational Medicine (TRANSMED) 
Agreement on Master’s thesis supervision 
 
STUDENT Last name Liang First name Zhihan 
E-mail zhihan.liang@helsinki.fi Phone / mobile +358 451675007 
THESIS Language: English Scope: 30 cr 
Title: Nano-Pick: A Novel Method Enables Spatially Resolved Gene Expression Profiling of 
Tertiary Lymphoid Structures in Ovarian Cancer 
 
 
SUPERVISION 
 The responsible supervisor must hold at least a doctoral degree. 
The supervisor and his or her unit or research group must provide the student with the necessary 
material, tools and equipment. The supervisor must allocate time for scientific supervision and provide 
feedback and advice on academic writing. If for any reason the supervisor in unable to carry out his or 
her supervisory duties and this to a significant extent extends the agreed schedule, TRANSMED director 
must be informed. 
The student is committed to working with the supervisor and completing the work within the agreed 
timetable. Responsibility for the progress of the work rests primarily with the student . 
Responsible 
supervisor 
 
 
 Name and academic degree: Anniina Färkkilä, MD PhD 
Affiliation: Principal investigator in Färkkilä lab, Research Program in Systems Oncology 
(ONCOSYS), Faculty of Medicine, University of Helsinki 
E-mail: anniina.farkkila@helsinki.fi 
Phone: +358 505967786 
Additional

ärkkilä lab, Research Program in Systems Oncology 
(ONCOSYS), Faculty of Medicine, University of Helsinki 
E-mail: anniina.farkkila@helsinki.fi 
Phone: +358 505967786 
Additional 
supervisors (if 
any) Name and academic degree: Ada Junquera Mencía, MSc 
E-mail: ada.junquera-mencia@helsinki.fi 
Practices in 
supervision 
(guidance, 
follow-up, 
contact with 
supervisors, 
feedback on 
writing) Supervisors will provide detailed guidance on the research methodology and help formulate the 
thesis title and research plan. Ongoing guidance and suggestions will also be offered during the 
experimental and data analysis phases. 
We agree to hold regular meetings to review experimental progress, address any challenges, 
and ensure the project remains on schedule. 
The student can contact supervisors in the working application Slack every working day or make 
an appointment before to meet and discuss in face. The student is encouraged to initiate 
contact for any queries or issues that arise, and supervisors commit to being accessible and 
providing timely responses. 
Supervisors will also offer constructive feedback on written work and help with revisions.
